scope: Actual LightningIndexer compressed-dKV diagnostic that precomputes each selected route entry's compact active-list slot, then reruns the sparse-local dot-K accumulator.
read: dot_k_slot_sparse isolates whether the failed dot_k_sparse body was mainly paying to find the active row with groups == selected_group. It precomputes route_slots[token,k] as the active-list row index, then uses offs_u == slot inside the Triton sparse-local accumulator. The result improves S128 and S512 only slightly, so active-row lookup is not the dominant mechanism. The scalar per-selected local accumulation loop remains the wall; the next branch needs segmented/MFMA-friendly reduction or LI/compressor fusion, not more Triton local sparse-loop cleanup.

decision.why: Precomputing active-list row slots changes S512 from 8.226 ms to only 8.062 ms. That is not enough to challenge dot_k_atomic at 0.272 ms or dense-route dot_k at 0.416 ms for the same S512 shape.
decision.s4096_run: skipped because S512 remains decisively negative.
decision.mechanism_read: The bottleneck is the scalar per-selected sparse accumulation body, not active-row lookup. Coalesced writes still need a segmented/MFMA-friendly reduction schedule or deeper LI/compressor fusion.
